topic_title: Foundation models — trained once at scale, usable for many tasks
verdict: PASS (cycle 2) — all hard checks clear. Citation density in section 7 (Real-World Patterns) rose from ~38% (cycle 1 fail) to ~80% after fixes to Claude, DALL-E/Stable Diffusion/Midjourney, Whisper, Krutrim entries; section 5 Core Concepts remains ~80%. Required sections present and non-trivial; no sequence/forward-reference drift; word count ~2000w within 1500–4400 standard range; no scope creep found. Four soft findings carry forward to HITL #2/#3: (1) 'open-weights' at line 133 still lacks an inline gloss (revision instruction not applied), (2) 'corpus' at line 130 still undefined for beginners, (3) section 5.3 lines 81–82 generalisation paragraph still uncited (section-level density passes at ~80%), (4) GPT-4o multimodal entry (line 139) and India government/research entry (line 144) remain uncited (section 7 overall density still 80%, above 60% threshold). Beginner readability soft-finding count remains at 2 — below the ≥3 escalation threshold.

uncited_claims: Section 5.3 lines 81–82: 'Because the patterns that a model learns from enormous, diverse data are not narrow rules. They are deep representations of meaning, structure, and relationships… It develops an understanding of how arguments are structured…' — still no citation marker. Revision instruction from cycle 1 not applied. Soft finding only — section-level density remains ~82%.

section_6_implementation.note: Not applicable — placeholder section with no factual assertions.

citation_density.overall_result: All hard-check sections (5, 7) above 60% threshold. Primary resource cited throughout. No fake citations.

notes: fine-tuning (lines 113, 153): named-and-deferred with explicit 'covered in topic 4.2' pointer — accepted.
RAG / Retrieval-Augmented Generation (lines 114, 166): named-and-deferred with 'covered in topic 4.3' pointer — accepted.
open-weights (line 133): not a future-topic concept; not a sequence-drift violation. Flagged as readability soft finding only.
knowledge cutoff (line 158): immediately explained inline — accepted.

length_sanity.note: ~2000 words (body text, excluding frontmatter). Within the task-specified 1500–4400 standard range. If the SKILL.md strict 3000–4400 range were applied, this corpus would fall below the lower bound; flagged for author awareness but not treated as a hard fail per the task-level specification.

topic_scope.headline: Foundation models — trained once at scale, usable for many tasks
findings: Section 5.4 names prompting, fine-tuning, and RAG as adaptation categories, each with one definitional sentence and an explicit 'covered later' pointer. Meets the allowed named-and-deferred pattern.
Best Practices (line 153) references fine-tuning in a comparative sentence to illustrate training data scope; no mechanics taught. Accepted.
Hands-On Exercise (line 166) previews the week-4 RAG lab as a forward pointer only; no RAG mechanics explained. Accepted.

findings.detail: 'open-weights' (line 133) still used without a plain-language inline gloss. Cycle 1 revision instruction (add gloss e.g. 'open-weights — meaning the model's internal settings are publicly released so anyone can download and run it') was not applied.
'corpus' (line 130) used in 'a vast corpus of text and code' without a definition. Borderline — arguably general academic vocabulary, but could confuse a true beginner.

beginner_readability.note: 2 distinct soft findings — below the threshold of 3 required to escalate to hard fail. Surfaces to HITL #2/#3 for author awareness.

soft_findings: beginner_readability.undefined_terms: open-weights (line 133) — inline gloss not added
